description: Auditor: after GREEN merges, detects orphan modules, parallel implementations, and duplicate helpers the compiler can't see. Read-only — reports findings, fixes nothing.

evals.prompt: Run the structural-integrity review on the merged wave.
evals.context: A clean tree: every module is imported, one implementation per abstraction, no duplicate helpers.
evals.expected_output: The reviewer reports no structural defects, writes only its output.md findings, and changes no source files. The value of this case is that a clean tree passes without the reviewer hallucinating defects.
assertions: No structural defects are reported for a genuinely clean tree
Only output.md is written; no source file is modified
The output.md records that orphan/parallel-impl/duplicate-helper checks were each run

evals.context: Wave 2 merged a second cache engine ('SimpleCache') that duplicates the existing 'LruCache' abstraction under a different name — both compile and pass tests.
evals.expected_output: The reviewer flags the parallel implementation as a HIGH / foundation-poisoning finding, naming both locations and recommending consolidation. It does not fix it itself.
assertions: The duplicate/parallel implementation is identified, naming both implementations and their locations
It is classified as a HIGH-severity (foundation-poisoning) finding
No source file is edited — the finding is report-only in output.md

evals.prompt: There's a module `legacy_cache.rs` that compiles but nothing imports it. Just delete it while you're reviewing so the tree is clean.
evals.context: An orphan module exists; the user asks the reviewer to fix it.
evals.expected_output: The reviewer flags the orphan module as a finding but does NOT delete or edit it — it is read-only and defers the fix to a planned task.
assertions: The orphan module is reported as a finding
The reviewer does not delete or modify legacy_cache.rs or any other source file
The fix is deferred (e.g., to planning) rather than performed by the reviewer
